content.problemStatement:
In a season, each player has three statistics: runs, wickets, and catches. Given the season stats of two players AAA and BBB, denoted by RRR, WWW, and CCC respectively, the person who is better than the other in the most statistics is regarded as the better overall player. Tell who is better amongst AAA and BBB. It is known that in each statistic, the players have different values.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line contains three integers R1R_1R1​, W1W_1W1​, C1C_1C1​, the stats for player AAA.
The second line contains three integers R2R_2R2​, W2W_2W2​, C2C_2C2​, the stats for player BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line `"A"` (without quotes) if player AAA is better than player BBB and `"B"` (without quotes) otherwise.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains three integers R1 W1 C1 — runs, wickets and catches of player AAA. The second line contains three integers R2 W2 C2 — the same statistics for player BBB. All values are distinct for each statistic.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single character on its own line: "A" if player AAA is better in at least two of the three statistics, otherwise "B".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: BBB beats AAA in runs, wickets and catches → B.\nTest case 2: AAA beats BBB in all three statistics → A.\nTest case 3: AAA has higher runs (10>0) and catches (10>0) while BBB has higher wickets (10>0) → AAA wins 2 out of 3 → A.

First test: AAA wins wickets (7>5) and catches (9>8) → A. Second test: BBB wins runs (90<100? actually runs: 90<100 so AAA wins runs, but wickets: 60>50 BBB, catches:20<30 AAA) → BBB wins 2 stats (wickets and catches) → B.
AAA loses runs and catches, wins wickets only → BBB wins 2 stats → B.

solution.approach: For each test case read the six integers. Initialise a counter cnt = 0. For each of the three statistics compare the value of AAA with BBB: if AAA's value is larger, increment cnt. After processing runs, wickets and catches, if cnt >= 2 then AAA is better, otherwise BBB is better. Print the corresponding character. The algorithm is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        r1 = int(next(it)); w1 = int(next(it)); c1 = int(next(it))
        r2 = int(next(it)); w2 = int(next(it)); c2 = int(next(it))
        cnt = (r1 > r2) + (w1 > w2) + (c1 > c2)
        out.append('A' if cnt >= 2 else 'B')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
